An amazing, extremely successful day that ended with a lovely dinner!  Spending time with customers like @[DHI Mortgage](urn:li:organization:163440) strengthens the partnership that drives our work forward.

The best solutions come from collaboration, trust, and shared goals.

We are all excited to keep our momentum moving fast-forward!

Mark Winter, Jason Stafin, Diane Yu, Tim Burdett, David Gackstetter, Jennifer Hedgepeth, Marcy Lindhout, CMB, G. Aaron Galloway, Daniel Farnum, Angela Hernandez, Shelly Brice, Tammi Jolly, Chad Wells, Mirela Tricic, Jason Cao, @[Chris McLendon](urn:li:person:DIJAwNQFR8), Shawna Johnson, Drew Schutz, Brian K.

Three sprints. Six weeks. Most of what shipped, nobody will ever see.

Six weeks ago, Credit Assist was in beta — opt-in for pilot lenders. Today, it’s live across our customer base, automatically reviewing every credit report against the appropriate agency guidelines the moment a loan is submitted or a new pull comes back.

Six weeks ago, the SOLO Workspace held two agents. Today it holds three — Asset Assist, Credit Assist, and Document Assist — with a unified task card layout that puts the key findings up top and the next action one click away.

Six weeks ago, a borrower upload triggered an immediate screening job. Today, SOLO waits for the upload session to settle, consolidates the work into a single analysis, and skips re-screening data that no longer applies.

This is what AI maturation in mortgage looks like.

Not a louder demo. Not a new model number. Just the steady accumulation of improvements that turn an AI feature into AI infrastructure — better extraction, smarter orchestration, cleaner handoffs between agents, and more work handled before a loan officer ever has to think about it.

The next phase of AI in mortgage will belong to the companies focused on operational trust, consistency, and workflow adoption. The ones whose AI gets quieter with every release because the experience simply works.

Three sprints down. The list keeps getting longer. And the loans keep moving.
Proud of the team behind every sprint, every iteration, and every improvement that makes the platform stronger for our customers.

Loan officers lose hours every week chasing paystubs, W-2s, bank statements, and employment letters. Borrowers send the wrong file. Documents are out of date. Someone forgets to follow up.

SOLO is built to take that work off the table.

When a borrower links their payroll provider or bank account during application, income, employment, and assets verify through @[Argyle](urn:li:organization:18936735), @[Plaid](urn:li:organization:2684737), @[Truv](urn:li:organization:68059038), or AccountChek by @[Informative Research](urn:li:organization:37573) — and the data lands in a form AUS can actually use.

A few things this changes for the team:

▶️ Verified data is ready the moment the LO needs it. No follow-up calls for the same documents three days in a row.

▶️ Borrowers get asked for the right things at the right time. A VA borrower with 0% down isn't asked to upload assets they don't need. A refi borrower without a reserve requirement skips the question entirely.

▶️ When income or assets are verified through a vendor, it's labeled that way — so the LO, the underwriter, and the borrower all know what's confirmed and what's still outstanding.

Less chasing. Fewer surprises at submission. More time on the work that actually moves a loan forward.

A 2025 report\* found that 11.5% of US mortgage loan file content has contained errors or missing information over the past decade. That translates to an estimated $7.8 billion in increased costs for consumers.

Bad data enters the pipeline. Weeks later, someone cleans it up.

Tidalwave's Document Assist agent reviews every upload in real time. It extracts data with 98%+ accuracy using mortgage-specific knowledge, catches name variations and typos with smart matching, flags mismatches across documents, and rejects files that don't meet quality standards at the source.

Bad data never enters the system.

Skeptical? Get a demo of how @[Tidalwave](urn:li:organization:94119582) works here: https://hubs.ly/Q04dXNmj0

\*Source: HousingWire, 7/29/25 - https://hubs.ly/Q04dXRFb0

📰 A big milestone, one we're glad to see getting some coverage. @[National Mortgage Professional](urn:li:organization:262393) covered @[Tidalwave](urn:li:organization:94119582)'s completed rollout with @[First Colony Mortgage](urn:li:organization:90026), one of our most innovative customers.

This one means a lot to us.

First Colony has been with Tidalwave since 2025. Our first target was wholesale, when 700-page broker packages \(the ugliest job in lending\) and "stare and compare" was the only way through. We learned the workflow together. They challenged what the product could be. We rebuilt parts of it because of what they taught us. And we didn't stop there.

Now SOLO is running both wholesale and retail at First Colony — dissecting files, running dual AUS successfully, tracking conditions, and validating borrower income and assets through multiple integrated third parties. The kind of work that used to require ten manual touches now moves in real time.

As Corey Shelley, owner & chairman of First Colony Mortgage, put it: "The mortgage industry has been stuck in a manual loop for decades. This allows us to move in real time."

Companywide AI is not an over-and-done press release moment. It's a thousand decisions about how a lender actually wants to operate.

First Colony made those decisions deliberately, alongside our team, and built something that works for the way their people work. The companies that get AI right in this cycle won't be the ones with the loudest launch. They'll be the ones who did the work.

Read the full story: https://hubs.ly/Q04hmP\_30 {hashtag|\#|mortgage} {hashtag|\#|AIinmortgage} {hashtag|\#|mortgagetech}

@[Tidalwave](urn:li:organization:94119582) and @[Columbia University](urn:li:organization:2624)'s DAP Lab ran the first public benchmark on mortgage AI accuracy in March 2026. On boolean verification questions, the yes/no checks that decide whether a loan gets flagged, @[Tidalwave](urn:li:organization:94119582)'s AI Agent SOLO scored 95%. \@ Claude 4.5 scored 42%.

The gap is not about model size. It is about context.

@[Tidalwave](urn:li:organization:94119582)'s patent-pending Mortgage Contextualizer maintains a structured knowledge graph of every loan from day one. Every document, every conversation, every decision, every borrower attribute, all connected. When SOLO answers an underwriting question, it is grounded in the actual file and the actual agency rules that govern it, not a probabilistic guess from training data.

Purpose-built for mortgage beats retrofit-to-mortgage. The numbers finally prove it.

Lenders, see it to believe it. Book a call today to learn more.

Most mortgage tech sales conversations start with a buyer who's already been burned by "innovation." They've heard the AI pitch. They've bought it. They've watched it sit on a shelf.

@[Chris McLendon](urn:li:person:DIJAwNQFR8), @[Tidalwave](urn:li:organization:94119582)'s CRO, sat down with Carter Armendarez, host of the Tech Sales podcast to talk about what it actually takes to sell AI-native technology into an industry running on margin pressure, legacy systems, and a deep allergy to hype.

A few things that landed:
• Lenders aren't shopping for AI. They're shopping for relief from $600 per-loan losses, origination costs up 35%, and 43-day close times. AI is the means, not the message.
• Pilots that sit on a shelf are worse than no pilot. The bar is production, in regulated workflows, with measurable ROI.
• Founder track record is doing real work in the sales cycle. Buyers want to know who built it before they care what it does.

Worth a listen if you're selling anything into mortgage right now, or if you're a lender tired of vendors who lead with the wrong thing. Full episode: https://hubs.ly/Q04dYF3T0

Mortgage is one of the biggest financial decisions most people will ever make, but the process still depends on slow workflows, heavy paperwork, and outdated systems. 

Diane Yu, @[Tidalwave](urn:li:organization:94119582)'s co-founder and CEO, sat down with Amir Bormand on @[The Tech Trek](urn:li:organization:42830970) to talk about what it actually takes to put agentic AI into a regulated industry without breaking it.

Diane explains why agentic AI in financial services has to be built differently. It is not enough to wrap a generic model around an old process. The system has to understand the domain, create transparency, log decisions, and know when to bring a human into the conversation. They got into:

• Why generic LLMs fail in regulated industries, and what changes when the system is built for the domain.
• How AI-native companies use their own tools internally to move faster than incumbents 50x their size.

Worth a listen if you're trying to figure out where AI actually moves the needle in financial services, and where it's still slideware.

If you’re in the mortgage industry, this is a must listen: https://hubs.ly/Q04dXgBZ0

Most mortgage delays aren't underwriting problems. They're data problems that show up at underwriting.

❌ A name that doesn't match across documents. A deposit that wasn't explained at application. An inconsistency no one caught until a processor three weeks in flagged it as a condition.

By then, the borrower is frustrated, the loan officer is scrambling, and another week is added to the timeline.

@[Tidalwave](urn:li:organization:94119582)'s agentic AI, SOLO, catches those problems at entry. Mortgage-specific extraction at 98%+ accuracy. Name mismatches, document inconsistencies, and data gaps flagged before they ever reach a processor.

✅ A file that arrives at underwriting clean moves differently than one that doesn't. That's how processing time drops from 43 days to under two weeks.

Lenders: book a demo to see what a clean pipeline looks like from day one. https://lnkd.in/eZuZfdR2

A borrower who speaks limited English has a 1 in 3 chance of abandoning their mortgage application. Not because they can't afford the home. Because the process was never built for them.

23% of Hispanic borrowers cite language as a barrier. Only 17% received any translation support from their lender. Close to 5 million U.S. households have limited English proficiency, and most mortgage platforms still treat that as someone else's problem.

@[Tidalwave](urn:li:organization:94119582)'s agentic AI, SOLO, provides full mortgage guidance in over 30 languages. Not a translated PDF. Not a Spanish-speaking staff member who may or may not be available. Real-time explanation of mortgage terminology, specific to that borrower's situation, at any hour.

For lenders, that's a market segment your current platform is likely losing. You don't need to hire to serve it. Book a demo to see how @[Tidalwave](urn:li:organization:94119582) works: https://lnkd.in/eZuZfdR2

▶️ Task automation for loan officers and underwriters: @[TidalWave](urn:li:organization:94119582)’s SOLO™ uses @[Plaid](urn:li:organization:2684737) and AccountCheck to validate income, employment checks, and tax form transcription, freeing loan officers from mundane tasks and allowing them to focus on closing more loans

@[TidalWave](urn:li:organization:94119582) is excited to announce the launch of a Spanish-language integration to our AI-powered platform, SOLO™. This enhancement enables lenders to provide a fully Spanish-speaking communication experience throughout the mortgage process—from application and processing to status updates and closing.

Hispanic Americans are a significant and growing segment of the U.S. housing market, with Spanish-speaking immigrants alone holding $1.7 trillion in buying power. Despite this, they remain underserved due to systemic barriers.

With TidalWave’s Spanish-language integration, lenders can effectively engage this vital market, unlocking opportunities for increased homeownership, improved efficiency, and closing the equity gap.

This launch marks the first step in our multilingual roadmap and underscores TidalWave’s commitment to empowering underserved communities. Together, we can transform the mortgage experience for Spanish-speaking borrowers, ensuring language is no longer a barrier to homeownership.

For more information about TidalWave’s Spanish AI integration, see our press release: https://lnkd.in/eYvAnq5R
